event.name: Lunar New Year Tết
event.dates: Feb 13–14, 2027 · 11 AM–5 PM · to be finalized
event.attendanceNote: Planning baseline ~6,000 attendees over the weekend

hero.headline: Help build the 2027 festival
hero.intro: Lunar New Year Tết returns to Elk Grove Regional Park this February — welcoming the Year of the Goat with a free, family-centered festival weekend: carnival, lion dance, kids programming, and local live music. Vendor fees fund the essentials. Sponsors unlock the magic.
hero.subhead: We're looking for directors — one owner per piece of the festival. Pick the lane you want to lead.
hero.body: We need directors — people who own an outcome, build a small team, and see it through with co-chair support. Co-chairs Thuy Pham (VACOS) and Bao Thien Ngo (cPALSs) back every lane.

steps: Pick one role (or two if they're closely related — we'll talk). See the full list on Get involved.
Apply on Idealist — name your role, preferred contact method (email, text, or phone), and any events you're already committed to this season.
We'll follow up within a few business days — add you to the staff directory, introduce vendor partners or teammates, and set a first check-in.
apply.idealistFallback: Can't use Idealist? Email contact@eglny.com with the role name and one sentence on why you're interested.

directorIntro.title: What is a director?
paragraphs: A director is the go-to person for one festival lane. You don't have to do every task yourself — you own the plan, coordinate vendors and volunteers, and report status to the co-chairs. Most roles are planning now through February 2027; peak workload is the two weeks before and event weekend.

Already covered: Festival co-chairs only — Thuy Pham (VACOS) and Bao Thien Ngo (cPALSs). Co-chairs hold coalition partnership-level decision authority; every lane director is open for 2027 recruitment.

23 director roles are open across site & ops, program & experience, and outreach & partners. Member and support roles fill in behind each director. Returning 2026 volunteers are welcome to apply — nothing is assumed until confirmed on the staff directory.
notes: Youth Culture Day (school multicultural rallies on stage) and Tết Relay Cup (open-age relay bracket on grass) are separate lanes with separate directors.
Parade & Procession is a sponsor-gated Phase 2 lane — likely cut at MVP unless a registry block funds it.
Saturday 5–8 PM headliner is sponsor unlock, not MVP baseline.
Volunteers Director owns shifts; if a critical lane stays unfilled past the steering trigger date, a contractor fallback may be approved.

lanes.title: Site & operations
lanes.subtitle: Keep the festival running
lanes.intro: Physical infrastructure, finance, and day-of execution. Vendor revenue funds this baseline — directors work with booked vendors and co-chairs on contracts and event weekend.

roles.title: Finance & Administration
roles.own: Budget, AP, vendor payments, post-event closeout
roles.fit: Are detail-oriented and comfortable tracking money in and out

roles.title: Foundation
roles.own: Insurance, CCSD/City permits, ops tents, contingency
roles.fit: Like checklists, deadlines, and make-it-official work

roles.title: Operations & Logistics
roles.own: Site map, load-in/out, day-of ops radio, Capitol Tent City
roles.fit: Love maps, timelines, and where does the truck go?

roles.title: Security
roles.own: Guard schedule (Sat–Sun festival hours)
roles.fit: Are calm under pressure; can liaise with Golden Protection

roles.title: Amenities
roles.own: Restrooms, ADA unit, hand-wash placement, porter service
roles.fit: Think about guest comfort, site flow, and stocked units through Sunday close

roles.title: Power & Lighting
roles.own: Generators, light towers, electrical zones
roles.fit: Can coordinate Rental Guys delivery and diesel logistics

roles.title: Parking & Transport
roles.own: Parking lots, overflow, golf carts
roles.fit: Can coordinate lot assignments, signage, and transport for a large regional park event

roles.title: Carnival Logistics
roles.own: Fun & Game profit-share, operator liaison, zero ops bleed
roles.fit: Can negotiate with carnival operators and keep rides off the ops budget

lanes.title: Program & experience
lanes.subtitle: What families see and remember
lanes.intro: Stages, schedule, and signature moments — including sponsor-funded features from Fund The Festival. Directors shape the plan, support sponsor asks when needed, and run the feature when funded.

roles.title: Program & Entertainment
roles.own: MC scripts, talent cues, and master schedule for outdoor + indoor stages

roles.title: Main Outdoor Stage
roles.own: Outdoor stage tent, sound, Saturday daytime programming (AFG lot)

roles.title: Indoor Stage (KP International)
roles.own: KP International tent · borrow terms · indoor sound · Chinh Kieu vendor liaison

roles.title: Youth Culture Day
roles.own: School outreach · Sunday run-of-show · MC intros · group hospitality
roles.fit: Know high school advisors, club presidents, or multicultural student programming — this is the stage showcase, not the relay cup

roles.title: Tết Relay Cup
roles.own: Bracket ops · corral layout · prizes · registration
roles.fit: Can run tournament logistics — sports, rec league, or festival games background

roles.title: Children's Show
roles.own: Talent booking · MC coordination · children's run-of-show

roles.title: Children's Village
roles.own: Village layout · crafts · gym/PTA activations · bounce houses or inflatables when funded
roles.fit: Can juggle family programming and two different play models — rent vs owned asset + labor

roles.title: Honor our Elders
roles.own: Rest zone layout · elder tea station staffing (sponsor-driven)

roles.title: Lion Dance & Heritage
roles.own: Lion team booking · 2× weekend performances

roles.title: Parade & Procession
roles.own: Performer booking · staging · route plan (registry-gated)

lanes.title: Outreach & partners
lanes.subtitle: Get people there and build allies

roles.title: Sponsorship
roles.own: Corporate pipeline, registry asks, sponsor deliverables
roles.fit: Can build business relationships and follow through on sponsor commitments

roles.title: Marketing & Communications
roles.own: Email, Instagram, website, organic promotion
roles.fit: Write clearly and want Elk Grove families to find us

roles.title: Vendor Sales
roles.own: Pipeline, category caps, profitability playbook
roles.fit: Can sell booth space fairly and hit category mix targets

roles.title: Vendor Operations
roles.own: Eventeny/Zeffy admin, booth layout handoff, TFF pass-through
roles.fit: Are detail-oriented and fair — vendor committee experience a plus

roles.title: Volunteers
roles.own: Recruitment, shifts, waivers, appreciation event
roles.fit: Know how to mobilize people and keep shifts covered

roles.title: Partnerships & Grants
roles.own: CCSD, City, SMUD, Yocha Dehe, grant renewals
roles.fit: Build institutional relationships; comfortable with grant language

phase2.title: Sponsor unlocks — Phase 2
phase2.intro: These director roles activate when sponsors fund the matching Fund The Festival gift. Plan with the base director in the meantime; hold the vision until the block unlocks.
roles: Saturday Night Headliner Director — Sat 5–8 PM B-tier anchor
Spread the Word Director — Paid Meta / Instagram reach
Decor & Cây Nêu Director — Community pool + decor backlog

faq.a: Helpful, not required. LNY 2026 runbooks and co-chairs back you up.

These are volunteer leadership roles. Sponsor-funded experiences may include modest stipends for performers or facilitators — not director compensation. If a lane stays unfilled past the steering trigger date, co-chairs may approve a contractor fallback from the ops budget.
One name on the directory as Director; co-leads listed as Members is fine.

faq.q: What if my experience isn't funded yet?
faq.a: Plan anyway — directors help pitch sponsors and hold the vision until the block unlocks. Parade and Saturday night headliner are explicitly sponsor-gated.

faq.q: What's the difference between site & ops and program?
faq.a: Site & ops keeps the gates open, power on, and guests comfortable. Program & experience is what families come for — stages, Tết Relay Cup, children's programming, and elder hospitality.
Planning baseline ~6,000 attendees over the weekend — a family fair, not a ticketed night market. Free gate, daylight hours (11 AM – 5 PM baseline).

faq.q: I'm already helping with Mid-Autumn Festival 2026 — can I do both?
faq.a: Yes — tell us your season commitments on Idealist and we'll coordinate lanes so nothing collides.

sections.title: Tết
paragraphs: Lunar New Year, known as Tết in Vietnamese culture, is one of the most significant and cherished celebrations in Asian communities worldwide. This ancient festival marks the beginning of the lunar calendar year and symbolizes renewal, family reunion, and hope for prosperity. For Vietnamese families, Tết represents a time to honor ancestors, strengthen family bonds, and welcome good fortune for the year ahead. Traditional celebrations include preparing special foods, decorating homes with flowers and symbols of luck, exchanging red envelopes (lì xì) for good fortune, and gathering with loved ones to reflect on the past year while embracing new beginnings.

For Vietnamese and Asian communities that fled after the Fall of Saigon following decades of war, arriving in America meant starting over in an unfamiliar land. As refugees worked to rebuild their livelihoods and establish new roots, Lunar New Year celebrations served a crucial function — providing a place of familiarity and community connection in their adopted homeland. This ancient tradition became more than just a cultural observance; it became an essential anchor that helped displaced families reorient themselves, maintain their identity, and forge bonds with fellow community members navigating similar challenges of resettlement.

sections.title: Sacramento & Elk Grove
paragraphs: Today, Lunar New Year Tết in Elk Grove builds on decades of cultural celebrations in the Sacramento region — from Stockton Boulevard and Florin to Elk Grove Regional Park. The festival is a free, family-centered gathering that welcomes the whole community: carnival, lion dance, multicultural programming, and live music.

The festival is co-hosted by Community Partners Advocate of Little Saigon Sacramento (cPALSs), Vietnamese American Community of Sacramento (VACOS), and Norcal Vietnamese Chamber of Commerce (NVCC), with generous support from sponsors, donors, and volunteers.
We hope you consider supporting our culturally-oriented and community-driven festival as part of this historical moment for our community.

posterWall.title: Festival posters over the years
posterWall.intro: Each season's festival poster reflects the zodiac year and the host neighborhood.
posterWall.note: 2027 Year of the Goat poster coming soon. Want to contribute art? Email contact@eglny.com.
